title: Probably raises $9M to build a more reliable kind of AI
slides: Most AI answers are wrong—what if they weren't?
Probabilistic models guess; Probably's AI verifies each step.
It caught a hallucination that three other AIs missed.
Your team can test now without retraining existing models.
Run one real query today and compare the confidence scores.
thread_text:
1. Most AI answers are wrong.

A recent test showed three different AIs all missed the same hallucination. One caught it.

2. Why they guess.

Probabilistic models predict the next likely word. They don't check if it's true.

3. Then one said stop.

Probably's AI verified each step and spotted a hallucination three others missed.

4. No retraining needed.

Your existing models stay as they are. You add verification on top.

5. Run one query today.

Take a real prompt your team uses. Compare confidence scores before and after. What will you find?

script: Probabilistic models guess the next word.
Probably's AI catches what others miss.
No retraining required for your models.
Test a real query and compare scores.

instagram.caption:
Three AIs all repeated the same hallucination. A fourth one caught it.

Probably just raised $9M to build verification into AI—checking each step instead of just guessing the next word. It works on top of your existing models, no retraining needed.

That means you can run one real prompt through it today and see confidence scores that flag errors before they reach your audience.

What would you find if you tested your team’s most-used query? 🤔🧠✅
